snippet.title: Amazon C.E.O. Andy Jassy on balancing labor, unions and profitability
snippet.description: Andy Jassy took the helm of Amazon in the midst of a global pandemic, a time when the company seemed more vital than ever to millions of people staying home. Since then Amazon has navigated labor issues, with plans to lay off thousands of employees and unionization efforts at several warehouses around the country. Andrew Ross Sorkin of The New York Times interviewed Jassy at the DealBook Summit event.

snippet.title: Welcome to Represent the Future (Day One)
snippet.description:
Andy Jassy delivers a welcome to this year's Represent the Future Summit.
Andy is President and CEO of Amazon.com and serves on the Board of Directors. He founded and led Amazon Web Services (AWS) from its inception and served as its CEO from April 2016 until July 2021. He joined Amazon in 1997 and, prior to founding AWS, held various leadership roles across the company, including both business-to-business and business-to-consumer.

snippet.title: Amazon CEO Sued For Union-Busting
snippet.description:
The National Labor Relations Board filed a suit against Amazon CEO Andy Jassy followed by his comments after workers in Staten Island voted to organize with the Amazon Labor Union. Jessica Burbank and Rayyvana discuss on Indisputable. Tell us what you think in the comments below. 

Read more here: https://www.washingtonpost.com/business/2022/10/26/amazon-nlrb-andy-jassy/

"The National Labor Relations Board filed a complaint on Wednesday alleging that Amazon CEO Andy Jassy violated labor law in two interviews he gave this year where he discussed his stance on unions at the e-commerce giant.

Jassy’s comments were made after workers in Staten Island voted to organize in April with the Amazon Labor Union, the first warehouse of the e-commerce giant to do so. This year, the NLRB has repeatedly found Amazon to have violated workers’ rights during a handful of unionization campaigns."

snippet.description:
#shorts
Amazon not close to accepting crypto, don't have Bitcoin myself: CEO

Amazon CEO Andy Jassy in an interview with CNBC said the e-commerce firm is not close to adding cryptocurrency as a payment option to its retail business. "I don't have Bitcoin myself," Jassy said. However, he said it might be possible for Amazon to sell NFTs and that he expects NFTs to continue to grow "significantly".

snippet.title: Andy Jassy: Amazon innovator or Jeff Bezos 2.0?
snippet.description:
Since Jeff Bezos stepped down last July, Andy Jassy has taken over as the new CEO of Amazon. He’s been challenged by slow profit growth, an Amazon Web Services (AWS) outage, and the company’s first union. But experts say he’s equipped to handle the challenges, just like his predecessor.

Rate this story: https://santv.co/3McQbuj.

“Jassy knows how Jeff thinks, Jassy knows how to run a meeting like Jeff runs meetings, he knows how to drive a huge complex organization like Jeff does,” said Bezonomics author Brian Dumaine, during an interview with Yahoo News. 

While he is relatively new to the CEO role, Jassy is not new to Amazon. He joined the company in 1997, when it was still selling books. He also trained under Bezos during a two year mentorship program.

Before leading the company, Jassy headed AWS, Amazon’s cloud computing platform and the company’s most profitable division. Though AWS isn’t without its challenges, Jassy had to discontinue its facial recognition software, ‘Amazon Rekognition’. The software was criticized for misidentifying people of color–an issue made worse after selling Rekognition to law enforcement agencies.

Like Bezos, Jassy has taken on projects outside of Amazon. While he’s not going into space anytime soon, he’s the chairman of a Seattle charter school and a part-owner of the NHL’s Seattle Kraken.

snippet.title: A Day In The Life of Jeff Bezos - Former Amazon CEO
snippet.description:
A Day In The Life of Jeff Bezos - Former Amazon CEO

Exactly 27 years and a healthy net worth after founding Amazon, Jeff Bezos left his position as CEO in July this year, handing over the reins to Andy Jassy and transitioning to become executive chairman.

In a letter to the company's 1.3 million employees dubbed 'Amazonians', the billionaire says he left his role as Chief Executive to focus his "energies and attention on new products and early initiatives."

Today, we'll run through Jeff Bezos' daily routine and take a look at how he grew Amazon from the cramped confines of his garage to become the world's largest online retailer.

snippet.description:

Jassy says that "...We happen to think they're better off not [unionizing].” Jassy goes on to make laughable claims that collective bargaining hurts “employee empowerment.”

snippet.title: Amazon CEO Hints at Future NFT Trading and Crypto Payments
snippet.description:
Amazon CEO Hints at Future NFT Trading and Crypto Payments

Amazon, the all-knowing god of retail, has set tongues wagging and whispers a-flying with its newest announcement that it may incorporate NFTs and cryptocurrency payments to its platform.

Andy Jassy, the newly appointed Amazon CEO, stated he was hopeful about the future of NFTs and cryptocurrency in a recent interview with CNBC. However, he did note that the technology will take some time to become operational on the company's retail platform.

snippet.title: Andy Jassy - CEO AWS
snippet.description:
Andy Jassy is President and CEO of Amazon.com and also serves on the Board of Directors. He founded and led Amazon Web Services (AWS) from its inception and served as its CEO from April 2016 until July 2021. He joined Amazon in 1997 and, prior to founding AWS, held various leadership roles across the company, including both business-to-business and business-to-consumer. He serves on the Trust of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, on the Board of Trustees for Rainier Scholars, and as Chair of Rainier Prep’s Board of Directors. He has an AB from Harvard University and an MBA from Harvard Business School.

snippet.title: Andy Jassy - The Best Business Building Quotes || CEO of Amazon || Best Quotes Official
snippet.description:
Andy Jassy - The Best Business Building Quotes || CEO of Amazon || Best Quotes Official
_______________________________________________________________________________________
Andrew R. Jassy is an American businessman and the CEO of Amazon Web Services (AWS). He is also one of the minority owners of the Seattle Kraken of thee National Hockey League. Jassy has led AWS since its inception in 2003. Jasssy will replace Jeff Bezos as CEO of Amazon in the third quarter of 2021; Bezos will become executive chairman. The CEO of AWS is also the chairman of Rainier prep, a charter school in Seattle.
The CEO of AWS is 53 years old as of January 2021.He was born on January 13, 1968 in Scarsdale in New York.
Jassy is the son of Margery and Everett L. Jassy of Scarsdale in New York. He is Jewish with Hungarian ancestry. His father was a senior partner in the corporate law firm Dewey Ballantine in New York, and chairman of the firm’s management committee. Jassy grew up in Scarsdale, and attended Scarsdale High school.
Jassy married in 1997. He married Elana Rochelle Caplan, a fashion designer for Eddie Bauer and graduate of the Philadelphia college of Textiles and science, at the Loews Santa Monica Beach Hotel. Their wedding ws officiated by New York Rabbi James Brandt, a cousin of Elena. Both their fathers were senior partners in law firm Dewey Ballantine. They have two children.
They live in the Capitol Hill neighborhood of Seattle, in a 10,000-square-foot house bought in 2009 for $3.1 million. In October 2020, it was reported that Jassy had bought a $6.7 million 5,500-square-foot house in Santa Monica, California, close to Bezos’ $165 million home in Beverly Hills.

snippet.title: Why Amazon’s CEO Is Working to Undo A Bezos-Led Overexpansion | WSJ Tech News Briefing
snippet.description:
Andy Jassy took over as Amazon CEO nearly a year ago. Since then the company’s stock price has fallen, online shopping demand has shrunk and the e-commerce giant has been left with excess warehouse space. As a result, Jassy has been forced to reverse course on some initiatives started by founder Jeff Bezos.

snippet.title: Amazon's CEO of Worldwide Consumer To Step Down After 23 Years || Hybiz tv
snippet.description:
Amazon's CEO of Worldwide Consumer To Step Down After 23 Years || Hybiz tv

Amazon.com Inc said on Friday the chief executive officer of its worldwide consumer business, David Clark, was leaving the company after 23 years of service to pursue other opportunities.Amazon CEO Andy Jassy said he expects to be ready with an update on Clark's succession over the next few weeks. Clark's last day in office would be July 1.Clark, who joined Amazon in 1999 right after graduating from his MBA program, has led teams that designed fulfillment centers and transportation networks.
He became the CEO of Worldwide Consumer, a unit that handles Amazon's global websites, in January last year when the company's e-commerce sales surged during the pandemic.Clark's departure is the second high-profile exit this week after Meta Platforms Inc's operations chief, Sheryl Sandberg, announced that she was leaving the company after 14 years.

snippet.title: Proposed New Senate Bill S.2992 Explained
snippet.description:
👉 155+ Things You Need to Succeed on Amazon (FREE Checklist): http://day2podcast.com

In his first solo podcast episode, Jason provides counterpoints to comments that Andy Jassy, CEO of Amazon, made in an interview at the Code 2022 conference.

The proposed Senate legislation, The American Innovation And Online Choice Act, popularly known as S.2992, has elicited mixed reactions from the big tech companies themselves, legislators, sellers, and consumers alike.

Amazon has particularly been very vocal in its opposition to this bill to the extent of running ads claiming that this proposed legislation will kill Prime as we know it.

Their CEO, Andy Jassy, was recently interviewed at the Code 2022 Conference, where he made some incorrect statements regarding the impact of this legislation on Amazon and on sellers on its platform.

He continues to claim that this bill unfairly targets Amazon, who he claims to be a 'small player’ in the retail space.

In this episode, I provide some counterpoints to Andy's comments and give my own insight on this bill based on my 20-year experience as an Amazon seller and 10 years of experience supporting sellers.

🔻 Subscribe so you never miss an episode 🔻
@avenue7media

- Intro (00:00)
- Is the bill broad, overreaching, and vague (03:13)
- The problem with fulfillment (05:33)

- Will this bill break the internet? (16:47)
- Amazon thinks they are a small retailer (18:32)

snippet.title: Andy Jassy Shareholder Letter 2021
snippet.description:
Andy Jassy, CEO of Amazon. Andy's first shareholder letter. The letter goes in the same narrative as Jeff Bezos talking about the growth of Amazon Marketplace, AWS, Prime, Devices, Labor, Climate Pledge. 

More importantly, the summary talks about 
- hiring the right kind of talent, 
- removing obstacles for the talent to make decisions, 
- better to fail and not ever tried, 
- innovate, 
- learn, 
- have a long term thinking and 
keep scaling. 

The letter has Andy Jassy's stamp with a reference to Foo Fighter - You Need Blind Faith, But No False Hope: This is a lyric from one of Andy's favorite Foo Fighters songs (“Congregation”)". 

You can find the song here - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AZHtZrfFuhU

You can find the Shareholder letter here - https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/company-news/2021-letter-to-shareholders

You can learn more about the $10bn bet with Project Kuiper on low orbit satellites to provide internet. Project Kuiper aims to provide Fast, Affordable Broadband for Unserved & Underserved Communities 
#ProjectKuiper #AmazonNews

snippet.title: Newish Amazon Web Service CEO Adam Selipsky: Insights on Amazon, AWS, Metaverse, Andy Jassy & more
snippet.description:
Adam Selipsky is the CEO of Amazon Web Services (AWS), the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud offering. He also is responsible for sustainability initiatives across Amazon, including achieving the company’s Climate Pledge commitments. Having previously led AWS marketing, sales, and support for 11 years, from 2005-2016, Selipsky helped launch and grow AWS from a start-up into a multi-billion dollar business. Prior to rejoining Amazon in 2021, Selipsky was most recently President and CEO of Tableau Software. He led Tableau through its acquisition by Salesforce, in what was the third largest software industry acquisition at the time. He has an AB in government from Harvard University and an MBA from Harvard Business School.
